rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of a DABCO-derived bromine complex (PTC1-Br2) from a benzyl-DABCO chloride salt via anodic oxidation of bromide in an undivided cell with HBr additive
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- (from NaBr) at the Pt anode generates Br2, which is sequestered by the DABCO nitrogen of PTC1 to form the PTC1-Br2 charge-transfer/N-Br2 complex; HBr additive is required to enable formation of the isolable yellow solid
electron_flow: At the Pt anode, Br- is oxidized to Br2; the tertiary amine of the DABCO-based PTC1 binds Br2 to give the PTC1-Br2 complex, with HBr additive protonating/balancing the system to enable productive complex isolation (without it only trace product forms).

probe_reagents.name: HBr additive (control: no additive vs 2 equiv HBr)
probe_reagents.observation: Without additive only trace PTC1-Br2 was obtained; with 2 equiv HBr the complex was isolated in 81% yield as a yellow solid

notes: This page documents the stoichiometric electrochemical preparation and isolation/characterization (1H, 13C NMR) of the proposed bromenium/Br2-DABCO intermediate (PTC1-Br2) implicated as the active brominating species in the parent catalytic reaction; HBr appears essential to stabilize/form the isolable complex.
